system_prompt: You are a senior web strategist at a digital agency specializing in SEO, conversion optimization, content strategy, and UX for marketing websites. You evaluate pages the way an agency would before a client proposal — looking for concrete wins, not theoretical advice. You are precise, analytical, and data-driven. ## Analysis Constraints 1. Analyze ONLY using the provided metrics and content — do not use external knowledge about the specific website. 2. You MUST reference specific metrics by name and value in every finding (e.g., "Word count of 300 is below the 600-word SEO threshold" or "4 out of 5 images (80%) lack alt text"). 3. Check for Heading Hierarchy Violations: H3 before any H2, missing H1, multiple H1s, level skips (H1 → H3). 4. Evaluate meta title length (ideal: 50–60 chars) and meta description length (ideal: 120–160 chars) using the provided character counts. 5. Consider rich visual media holistically: a page may lack traditional tags but use SVGs, CSS animations, video embeds, Lottie animations, canvas/WebGL, or 3D elements. Do NOT penalize visual quality if rich media alternatives are present. 6. Assess technical SEO signals: viewport meta, canonical URL, robots meta, Open Graph tags, Twitter Card, and structured data (JSON-LD) presence. 7. Evaluate CTA quality: are there enough CTAs for the page length? Are they above the fold? Is the language action-oriented? 8. For content depth: consider topical coverage, use of subheadings to organize content, internal/external link strategy, and whether the content matches likely search intent. 9. Do NOT make generic statements — every claim must cite a metric or content excerpt. 10. Scores must be integers from 1 to 10. ## Recommendation Constraints 11. Generate 5 recommendations in the "recommendations" array. Only reduce to 4 if the page is near-perfect in one category, or 3 if the page excels in multiple areas. Default to 5. 12. Every recommendation MUST be directly grounded in your analysis findings — never generic advice. 13. Prioritize by impact: priority 1 = highest impact, must-fix; priority 2 = significant improvement; priority 3 = nice-to-have optimization. 14. The "grounded_metric" field must cite the exact data point (e.g., "4 images missing alt text", "Meta title is 78 chars — exceeds 60-char ideal"). 15. The "action" field must be a specific, implementable step — not vague advice. 16. The "expected_impact" field must describe the concrete expected outcome. 17. Focus recommendations on the lowest-scoring categories first. ## Quality Guards 18. THINK BEFORE SCORING: For each category, first identify the relevant metrics, then compare against best practices, then assess real-world impact — only then assign a score. Never pick a score first and justify it after. 19. ANTI-HALLUCINATION: If the extracted metrics say a feature is MISSING (e.g., "Structured Data (JSON-LD): None" or "Canonical URL: MISSING"), treat it as definitively absent. Do not infer its presence from the page text. 20. NO DUPLICATE RECOMMENDATIONS: Each of the 5 recommendations must address a distinct issue. Do not suggest the same fix in different wording. 21. PROFESSIONAL TONE: Avoid vague marketing buzzwords ("leverage", "supercharge", "synergize"). Write like a senior consultant delivering a client audit report. - 9-10: Exceptional — best practices exceeded, clear competitive advantage - 7-8: Good — solid foundation with minor improvements possible - 5-6: Average — functional but missing key optimizations that impact rankings/conversions - 3-4: Below average — significant gaps that hurt performance - 1-2: Critical — fundamental issues that need immediate attention Return a single JSON object with: 1. Scores and detailed analysis for each of the 5 categories (structure, messaging, CTAs, content depth, UX) 2. A "recommendations" array with 5 prioritized, actionable recommendations grounded in your analysis (reduce to 4 or 3 only if the page truly excels) All in one structured response matching the provided schema. ## Good vs. Bad — calibrate your output quality BAD finding (vague, no data): "The content is too short and doesn't explain the product well." GOOD finding (grounded, specific): "Word count of 300 is below the 600-word threshold for competitive mid-funnel queries, and with no rich media alternatives (0 videos, 0 canvas elements), the page lacks depth to rank." BAD recommendation (generic): "Improve SEO by adding alt text to images." GOOD recommendation (actionable, cited): "Add descriptive alt text to the 4 images missing it (80% of total). Prioritize the hero banner and product thumbnails, as these appear in Google Image search results."
user_prompt: ## Extracted Page Metrics (Deterministic — scraped from HTML) ### Content Metrics - Word Count: 384 - CTA Count: 25 - Internal Links: 49 - External Links: 2 ### Image & Visual Media - Total Images (): 53 - Images Missing Alt Text: 0 (0.0%) - Images with Decorative Alt (alt=""): 51 - Rich Visual Media: Video embeds detected, CSS animations/transitions detected ### Technical SEO - Meta Title: B2B Digital Agency for Enterprises | eight25 (44 chars — ideal: 50-60) - Meta Description: Global B2B digital agency offering enterprise web design, mobile apps, and strategic consulting for Fortune 500 firms. (118 chars — ideal: 120-160) - Viewport Meta: Present - Canonical URL: Present - Robots Meta: Present - Open Graph Tags: Present - Twitter Card: Present - Structured Data (JSON-LD): None ### Heading Structure - Heading Counts: {"h1": 1, "h2": 5, "h3": 10, "h4": 0, "h5": 0, "h6": 0} ## Heading Hierarchy (order as found in HTML) [["H1", "Digital experiences for the modern enterprise"], ["H2", "The world’s best companies"], ["H2", "choose to work with eight25"], ["H3", "Google"], ["H3", "Qlik"], ["H3", "Intralinks"], ["H3", "Andela"], ["H3", "Qualcomm"], ["H2", "Insights"], ["H3", "Analyzing the ‘Request a Demo’ Page: Unpacking Why Conversions Are Lagging"], ["H3", "Uncovering Homepage Hero Friction: Why Visitors Bounce and How to Fix It"], ["H3", "Why Is Brand Alignment Important For B2B Enterprise Websites?"], ["H3", "Assessment:  Is your website working hard enough?"]] ## Page Content (visible text excerpt) Digital experiences for the modern enterprise We build brands and web experiences that help you build the ultimate customer journey. Learn More The world’s best companies choose to work with eight25 Google Qlik Intralinks Andela Qualcomm Insights Analyzing the ‘Request a Demo’ Page: Unpacking Why Conversions Are Lagging Read More Uncovering Homepage Hero Friction: Why Visitors Bounce and How to Fix It Read More Why Is Brand Alignment Important For B2B Enterprise Websites? Read More Assessment:  Is your website working hard enough? We’ve been privileged to work with Fortune 500 companies and funded startups alike, to build some of the most innovative solutions on the web. Review an in-depth assessment analyzing the strengths, weaknesses, and opportunities of your digital presence. Success stories… Let’s talk Name Email Phone Message Submit Perform a complete website audit in two parts: **STEP 0 — Page Classification**: First, determine the primary purpose of this page from its content, CTAs, and structure (e.g., SaaS landing page, e-commerce product page, blog/article, documentation, portfolio, corporate homepage). Tailor your scoring expectations to that page type — a blog post needs fewer CTAs than a landing page, and a portfolio site may rely on visuals over word count. **PART 1 — Analysis**: Evaluate the page across all five categories: - **Structure & SEO**: Heading hierarchy (H1 presence, proper nesting, no skips), meta tag quality (title/description length and relevance), technical SEO signals (viewport, canonical, OG tags, structured data), and overall HTML organization. - **Messaging & Clarity**: Value proposition clarity, brand voice consistency, whether the content answers the user's likely intent, and if key messages are prioritized above the fold. - **CTAs**: Quantity relative to page length, action-oriented language quality, variety of CTAs (primary vs secondary), and strategic placement signals. - **Content Depth**: Word count relative to topic complexity, use of subheadings for scannability, internal/external link strategy for topical authority, and whether the content demonstrates expertise (E-E-A-T signals). - **UX**: Content readability structure (short paragraphs, logical flow), mobile-readiness signals (viewport meta), image optimization (alt text), rich media usage for engagement, and overall page organization. For each category, provide a score (1-10), findings grounded in specific metrics, and direct evidence. **PART 2 — Recommendations**: Based on your analysis above, generate 5 prioritized recommendations (reduce to 4 or 3 only if the page truly excels in most categories). Focus on the lowest-scoring categories first. Each recommendation must cite specific data and be the kind a web agency would include in a client audit report. Return everything as a single structured JSON object matching the schema.

structure_analysis.findings: The page has a solid technical SEO foundation with Viewport Meta, Canonical URL, Robots Meta, Open Graph Tags, and Twitter Card all present. The heading hierarchy is well-structured with a single H1 and logical progression of H2s and H3s, without any skips (H1: 1, H2: 5, H3: 10). However, a significant technical SEO gap is the complete absence of Structured Data (JSON-LD). Furthermore, while 0 images are missing alt text, 51 out of 53 images (96.2%) are marked with alt="", indicating they are decorative. Many of these, such as client logos, are likely not decorative and should contain descriptive alt text for accessibility and SEO.
structure_analysis.evidence: Structured Data (JSON-LD): None. Images with Decorative Alt (alt=""): 51. Heading Counts: {"h1": 1, "h2": 5, "h3": 10}. Meta Title: 44 chars (ideal: 50-60). Meta Description: 118 chars (ideal: 120-160).

messaging_analysis.findings: The page clearly communicates its value proposition and target audience. The H1 'Digital experiences for the modern enterprise' immediately sets the tone, reinforced by the sub-headline 'We build brands and web experiences that help you build the ultimate customer journey.' The messaging consistently targets 'enterprises' and 'Fortune 500 firms,' aligning with the agency's stated focus. Strong social proof is provided by listing prominent client logos like Google, Qlik, and Qualcomm under the heading 'The world's best companies choose to work with eight25'. The navigation also clearly outlines the services offered, ensuring clarity on capabilities.
messaging_analysis.evidence: H1: 'Digital experiences for the modern enterprise'. Sub-headline: 'We build brands and web experiences that help you build the ultimate customer journey.'. H2: 'The world's best companies choose to work with eight25' followed by client names (Google, Qlik, Intralinks, Andela, Qualcomm). Meta Description: 'Global B2B digital agency offering enterprise web design, mobile apps, and strategic consulting for Fortune 500 firms.'

cta_analysis.findings: The page features a high volume of CTAs, with a total of 25, which is substantial for a word count of 384. While the CTA language is generally action-oriented ('Let's talk', 'Learn More', 'Read More', 'Submit'), the primary call-to-action for lead generation is not prominently placed above the fold immediately after the main value proposition. The first prominent CTA is 'View all work' after the client logos. A 'Learn More' CTA is present under 'Studio Services' which currently contains placeholder text, rendering that specific CTA ineffective.
cta_analysis.evidence: CTA Count: 25. 'Let's talk' in header. 'Learn More' under 'Studio Services' with placeholder content 'Cras mattis consectetur purus sit amet fermentum...'. No prominent primary CTA button immediately after 'We build brands and web experiences that help you build the ultimate customer journey.'.

content_depth_analysis.findings: The page's word count of 384 is relatively low for a corporate homepage targeting enterprise clients, which typically require more detailed information to build trust and demonstrate expertise. While rich visual media (video embeds, CSS animations) helps, the textual content could be expanded. The page effectively uses subheadings to organize content into sections like 'Insights' and 'Success stories,' which contribute to topical coverage and E-E-A-T signals. However, the presence of placeholder text ('Cras mattis consectetur purus sit amet fermentum...') under the 'Studio Services' section significantly detracts from content depth and professionalism.
content_depth_analysis.evidence: Word Count: 384. Page Content: 'Cras mattis consectetur purus sit amet fermentum. Nullam quis risus eget urna mollis ornare vel eu leo. Curabitur blandit tempus porttitor.' under 'Studio Services'. Internal Links: 49. External Links: 2. H2s for 'Insights' and 'Success stories...'.

ux_analysis.findings: The page demonstrates good UX principles with clear organization, scannable content broken into sections by headings, and the presence of a viewport meta tag indicating mobile-friendliness. Rich visual media, including video embeds and CSS animations, is effectively used to enhance engagement. The contact form at the bottom is clear and easy to use. However, a significant accessibility and potential UX issue is that 51 out of 53 images (96.2%) have empty alt attributes (alt=""). While technically not 'missing' alt text, marking nearly all images as decorative is problematic if they convey meaningful information (e.g., client logos, project visuals) to users relying on screen readers.
ux_analysis.evidence: Viewport Meta: Present. Images with Decorative Alt (alt=""): 51. Rich Visual Media: Video embeds detected, CSS animations/transitions detected. Heading Hierarchy: H1, H2, H3 used to structure content. Contact form present at the bottom.

recommendations.title: Implement Structured Data (JSON-LD)
recommendations.description: The page currently lacks any structured data, which is a critical missed opportunity for search engines to understand the agency's services, location, and organizational details. Implementing relevant schema can significantly improve search visibility and rich snippet potential.
recommendations.action: Add Organization schema, LocalBusiness schema (if applicable), and Service schema using JSON-LD to the page's HTML. Ensure all relevant fields are accurately populated.
recommendations.expected_impact: Improve search engine understanding of the agency, its services, and location, potentially leading to rich snippets in search results and enhanced local search visibility.

recommendations.title: Review and Add Descriptive Alt Text to Images
recommendations.description: While no images are technically missing alt text, 51 out of 53 images are marked as decorative (alt=""). Many of these, such as client logos or project visuals, are likely not decorative and convey important information. This impacts accessibility for screen reader users and limits image SEO potential.
recommendations.action: Conduct a thorough audit of all images. For images that convey meaning (e.g., client logos, video thumbnails, project screenshots), replace alt="" with descriptive alt text. Only use alt="" for truly decorative images that add no informational value.
recommendations.expected_impact: Enhance accessibility for users with visual impairments, improve image search engine optimization, and provide better context for images if they fail to load, contributing to a more inclusive user experience.

recommendations.title: Expand Content and Remove Placeholder Text
recommendations.description: The page's word count is low for an enterprise-focused agency homepage, and the presence of placeholder text under 'Studio Services' undermines professionalism and content depth. Expanding the content will provide more value to visitors and improve SEO signals.
recommendations.grounded_metric: Word Count: 384, Page Content: 'Cras mattis consectetur purus sit amet fermentum...' under Studio Services
recommendations.action: Replace the placeholder text under 'Studio Services' with actual, detailed descriptions of the services offered. Additionally, expand other key sections, such as service overviews or the 'About' section, to increase the overall word count and provide more comprehensive information.
recommendations.expected_impact: Improve content depth and authority for search engines, enhance the agency's credibility and professionalism, and provide more compelling information to potential clients, leading to increased engagement.

recommendations.title: Add a Prominent Primary CTA Above the Fold
recommendations.description: Despite having 25 CTAs, a clear, primary call-to-action is missing immediately after the main value proposition above the fold. This can lead to missed conversion opportunities as visitors may not immediately know the desired next step.
recommendations.grounded_metric: No immediate primary CTA button after main value proposition
recommendations.action: Introduce a prominent, distinct primary CTA button (e.g., 'Request a Consultation', 'Start Your Project') directly below the main sub-headline ('We build brands and web experiences that help you build the ultimate customer journey.') to guide visitors to the next conversion step.
recommendations.expected_impact: Increase conversion rates by providing a clear and immediate path for interested visitors to engage, reducing friction and improving lead generation efficiency.

recommendations.title: Optimize Meta Title for Keyword Expansion
recommendations.description: The current meta title is slightly shorter than the ideal character range, potentially missing an opportunity to include additional relevant keywords that could attract a broader audience and improve click-through rates from search results.
recommendations.grounded_metric: Meta Title: B2B Digital Agency for Enterprises | eight25 (44 chars)
recommendations.action: Revise the meta title to utilize more of the ideal 50-60 character limit. Consider incorporating a key service or a stronger benefit-oriented phrase, such as 'B2B Digital Agency for Enterprises | Web Design & Strategy | eight25'.
recommendations.expected_impact: Potentially improve organic click-through rates by providing more context and attracting a wider range of relevant search queries, enhancing the page's visibility in SERPs.
